purpose.summary: This COBOL program deletes authorization details and summary records from an IMS database. It also takes checkpoints to ensure data recovery in case of failure.
purpose.business_context: This program is likely part of a larger authorization processing system, responsible for removing pending authorization data after it has been processed or is no longer needed.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph deletes authorization detail records from the IMS database. It first checks if debugging is enabled and displays the account ID if it is (lines 4-6). It then executes a DLI DLET command to delete the PAUTDTL1 segment from the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS using the PAUT-PCB-NUM (lines 8-11). After the delete operation, it checks the DIBSTAT to determine if the deletion was successful (line 13). If DIBSTAT is spaces, it increments the WS-NO-DTL-DELETED counter (line 14). If the deletion fails (DIBSTAT is not spaces), it displays an error message with the DIBSTAT and PA-ACCT-ID, and then performs 9999-ABEND to terminate the program (lines 16-18).
This paragraph simply exits the 5000-DELETE-AUTH-DTL paragraph. It provides a standard exit point for the paragraph.

This paragraph deletes authorization summary records from the IMS database. It first checks if debugging is enabled and displays the account ID if it is (lines 29-31). It then executes a DLI DLET command to delete the PAUTSUM0 segment from the PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY using the PAUT-PCB-NUM (lines 33-36). After the delete operation, it checks the DIBSTAT to determine if the deletion was successful (line 38). If DIBSTAT is spaces, it increments the WS-NO-SUMRY-DELETED counter (line 39). If the deletion fails (DIBSTAT is not spaces), it displays an error message with the DIBSTAT and PA-ACCT-ID, and then performs 9999-ABEND to terminate the program (lines 41-43).
This paragraph simply exits the 6000-DELETE-AUTH-SUMMARY paragraph. It provides a standard exit point for the paragraph.

This paragraph takes a checkpoint using the IMS CHKP call. It issues the checkpoint call with an ID stored in WK-CHKPT-ID (lines 53-54). After the checkpoint call, it checks the DIBSTAT to determine if the checkpoint was successful (line 56). If DIBSTAT is spaces, it increments the WS-NO-CHKP counter (line 57). If the WS-NO-CHKP counter reaches the value in P-CHKP-DIS-FREQ, it resets the WS-NO-CHKP counter to zero and displays a checkpoint success message with the current authorization count (WS-NO-SUMRY-READ) and application ID (WS-CURR-APP-ID) (lines 58-62). If the checkpoint fails (DIBSTAT is not spaces), it displays an error message with the DIBSTAT, record count, and application ID, and then performs 9999-ABEND to terminate the program (lines 64-67).
This paragraph simply exits the 9000-TAKE-CHECKPOINT paragraph. It provides a standard exit point for the paragraph.

This paragraph handles program termination due to an error. It displays a message indicating that the program is abending (line 78). It then sets the RETURN-CODE to 16 (line 80) and uses GOBACK to terminate the program and return control to the calling environment.
This paragraph simply exits the 9999-ABEND paragraph. It provides a standard exit point for the paragraph, although it's unlikely to be used in practice given that 9999-ABEND terminates the program.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT NOT = SPACES during DELETE AUTH DETAIL
DIBSTAT NOT = SPACES during DELETE AUTH SUMMARY

open_questions.question: What is the purpose of the DEBUG-ON variable?
open_questions.context: The code checks for DEBUG-ON but its origin and how it's set are unclear.
open_questions.suggestion: Check the calling programs or related documentation for how DEBUG-ON is used.

open_questions.question: What is the purpose of P-CHKP-DIS-FREQ?
open_questions.context: The code uses P-CHKP-DIS-FREQ to determine when to display a checkpoint message, but its origin and how it's set are unclear.
open_questions.suggestion: Check the calling programs or related documentation for how P-CHKP-DIS-FREQ is used.
